title: Once viewed as a rental, could Viktor Arvidsson be ideal ‘bridge’ forward for Bruins?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Viktor Arvidsson is key to Bruins, ranks second in 5-on-5 goals despite pending free agent status.
Bruins bypassed trading Arvidsson for draft picks, highlighting his vital role in team dynamics and playoff push.
Arvidsson's potential extension as a bridge player could provide stability amid cap space and emerging young talent.

title: 5 interior offensive linemen options for Patriots after Garrett Bradbury trade
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots need a left guard after trading center Garrett Bradbury to the Bears.
Isaac Seumalo, 32, Steelers guard, is a top free agent option with a projected $8 million contract.
Emmanuel Pregnon, Oregon guard, is a draft prospect with an elite 9.42 Relative Athletic Score.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for the ‘amazing’ Make-A-Wish children
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jason Perry, 48, from Stockton, California, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon.
Perry runs to support Make-A-Wish Foundation kids in the Greater Massachusetts-Rhode Island region.
He has connected with Wish children over 15 years, inspired by their resilience and hope.

title: Insider predicts Patriots sign Seahawks standout pass rusher, but don’t land Alec Pierce in free agency
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Patriots predicted to sign Seahawks edge rusher Boye Mafe for three years, $48 million, with $25 million guaranteed.
Boye Mafe had 44 pressures and a 17.4% pass rush win rate last season, enhancing Seahawks' Super Bowl run.
ESPN's Dan Graziano forecasts the Chargers signing Colts' WR Alec Pierce with a three-year, $80 million deal.

title: Review & setlist: With sharp sarcasm, Jesse Welles took on war, bullies, and Trump at the House of Blues
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Jesse Welles performed at Boston's House of Blues, delivering sharp political commentary through music on his 'Under the Powerlines Tour.'
Welles was nominated for four Grammy Awards in 2026 for his work in folk and Americana music categories.
The concert featured 20 songs, including original works and covers of Black Sabbath, Radiohead, and John Denver.

title: Masataka Yoshida hits a home run in Japan’s win vs. Korea, with Drake Maye in attendance
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Masataka Yoshida hit a home run and had three RBIs in Japan's 8-6 win over Korea in the World Baseball Classic.
Yoshida's performance included a 387-foot home run and a two-run single, moving Japan to 2-0 in pool play.
Patriots quarterback Drake Maye was in attendance at the Tokyo Dome, coinciding with family activities in Japan.

title: This Roslindale native is running her first Boston Marathon for Mass. Eye and Ear
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Makayla Aboujaoude-Quirk, 25, from Roslindale, is running her first Boston Marathon in 2026.
She is running with Team Eye and Ear to support Mass Eye and Ear, where she's been a patient for nearly a decade.
Aboujaoude-Quirk is a Boston Public Schools teacher and is passionate about fundraising for the hospital's research and services.

title: It was overdue, but Tim Legler was promoted to ESPN/ABC’s top NBA broadcast team this season, and he’s thriving
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Tim Legler, ESPN/ABC's longest-tenured NBA analyst, was promoted to their top NBA broadcast team in August 2025.
Legler joins Mike Breen and Richard Jefferson to call high-stakes games, including the NBA Finals in June.
Legler's promotion follows personnel changes after Doc Rivers and JJ Redick left for coaching jobs, stabilizing the team.

title: Cooper Flagg was the Maine story in Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Chad Finn
bullets: Cooper Flagg scored 16 points, 8 rebounds, and 6 assists during his NBA debut at TD Garden against the Celtics.
Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics lineup with 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists after recovering from an Achilles tear.
Flagg, a Maine native and Mavericks rookie, admired Tatum as a mentor, having attended his summer camp and followed his Duke path.

title: Charles Barkley impressed by Jayson Tatum in return, but doesn’t view Celtics as favorites in East
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to play for the Celtics after a 298-day recovery from an Achilles tear, scoring 15 points.
Charles Barkley remains unconvinced about the Celtics' dominance despite Tatum's return, favoring the Cavaliers in the East.
Boston's 22 wins since January 1 are tied for the most in the NBA, matching the San Antonio Spurs.
